answer:
To evaluate the limit \(\lim_{{x \to 1}} \left((x - 1) \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{x - 1}\right)\right)\), let \(t = x - 1\), so the limit becomes \(\lim_{{t \to 0}} t \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{t}\right)\).

Since \(\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{t}\right)\) oscillates between \(-1\) and \(1\) as \(t \to 0\), the product \(t \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{t}\right)\) is squeezed between \(-|t|\) and \(|t|\), both of which tend to \(0\).

Thus, the limit is \(\boxed{B}\).
